title: Verify Custom EQ slider adjustments are saved and persisted after reopening EQ settings
steps: 1. Launch the application and navigate to the main screen.
2. Open Settings / Audio Settings / EQ Settings.
3. Select 'Custom EQ' mode (if not already selected).
4. Adjust at least one EQ slider (e.g., 60 Hz band) to a non-default value (e.g., +3 dB).
5. Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
6. Close the EQ settings (e.g., tap Back or close the settings panel).
7. Reopen Settings / Audio Settings / EQ Settings.
8. Observe the position of the previously adjusted EQ slider(s).
expected: The Custom EQ slider(s) should retain the previously saved values (e.g., 60 Hz band still at +3 dB) after reopening the EQ settings.

title: Verify multiple Custom EQ slider adjustments persist after app restart
steps: 1. Launch the application and navigate to Settings > Audio Settings > EQ Settings.
2. Select 'Custom EQ' mode.
3. Adjust multiple EQ sliders to distinct non-default values (e.g., 60 Hz: +5 dB, 230 Hz: -2 dB, 910 Hz: +1 dB, 4 kHz: -3 dB, 14 kHz: +4 dB).
4. Tap 'Save EQ' to persist the custom profile.
5. Close the EQ settings and fully close/terminate the application.
6. Relaunch the application.
7. Navigate to Settings > Audio Settings > EQ Settings.
8. Check the values of all previously adjusted EQ sliders.
expected: All Custom EQ slider values should match the saved profile (60 Hz: +5 dB, 230 Hz: -2 dB, 910 Hz: +1 dB, 4 kHz: -3 dB, 14 kHz: +4 dB) after app restart.

title: Verify 'Save EQ' button is functional and persists Custom EQ profile
steps: 1. Launch the application and go to Settings > Audio Settings > EQ Settings.
2. Select 'Custom EQ' mode.
3. Adjust one or more EQ sliders to non-default values.
4. Without tapping 'Save EQ', close the EQ settings.
5. Reopen EQ settings and verify slider positions are reset to defaults.
6. Adjust the same sliders again to the same non-default values.
7. Tap the 'Save EQ' button.
8. Close and reopen the EQ settings.
9. Observe the slider positions.
expected: Step 5: Without saving, slider positions should revert to defaults. Step 9: After tapping 'Save EQ', the slider positions should persist and match the saved values.

title: Verify switching away from Custom EQ and back retains saved profile
steps: 1. Launch the application and navigate to Settings > Audio Settings > EQ Settings.
2. Select 'Custom EQ' mode and adjust sliders to non-default values.
3. Tap 'Save EQ' to save the custom profile.
4. Switch to a different EQ preset (e.g., 'Rock', 'Pop', or 'Classical').
5. Verify the EQ sliders change to match the selected preset.
6. Switch back to 'Custom EQ' mode.
7. Observe the EQ slider positions.
expected: After switching back to 'Custom EQ' mode, the sliders should return to the previously saved custom values, not the default flat values.

title: Verify EQ slider values persist after device orientation change
steps: 1. Launch the application and navigate to Settings > Audio Settings > EQ Settings.
2. Select 'Custom EQ' mode.
3. Adjust EQ sliders to non-default values (e.g., 60 Hz: +2 dB, 14 kHz: +3 dB).
4. Tap 'Save EQ'.
5. Rotate the device from portrait to landscape (or vice versa).
6. Observe the EQ slider positions after rotation.
expected: The Custom EQ slider values should remain unchanged after device orientation change.

title: Verify EQ slider values persist after navigating away and back to settings
steps: 1. Launch the application and navigate to Settings > Audio Settings > EQ Settings.
2. Select 'Custom EQ' mode.
3. Adjust EQ sliders to non-default values.
4. Tap 'Save EQ'.
5. Navigate back to the main screen or another section of the app.
6. Navigate back to Settings > Audio Settings > EQ Settings.
7. Observe the EQ slider positions.
expected: The Custom EQ slider values should persist after navigating away from and back to the EQ settings screen.
